---
title: Redis
description: Production cache backed by ioredis.
icon: Server
---

## When to use

Use the Redis adapter for production deployments where you need persistent, shared cache state across multiple app instances.

## Installation

```bash
bun add @justwant/cache ioredis
```

## Setup

```ts title="lib/cache.ts"
import { createCache } from "@justwant/cache";
import { redisAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/redis";
import Redis from "ioredis";

const redisClient = new Redis(process.env.REDIS_URL!);

export const cache = createCache({
  adapter: redisAdapter({ redis: redisClient }),
});
```

## Options

```ts
redisAdapter({
  redis: RedisClient;     // ioredis (or compatible) client instance (required)
  keyPrefix?: string;     // Key prefix applied to all entries
  tagPrefix?: string;     // Prefix for tag set keys (default: "tag:")
})
```

| Option | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `redis` | `RedisClient` | ioredis (or compatible) client instance |
| `keyPrefix` | `string` | Prefix applied to every Redis key |
| `tagPrefix` | `string` | Prefix for tag set keys (default: `"tag:"`) |

## Environment variable

```bash
# .env
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
```

## Cluster support

Pass an ioredis `Cluster` instance to use Redis Cluster:

```ts
import { Cluster } from "ioredis";

const cluster = new Cluster([{ host: "127.0.0.1", port: 6379 }]);
const adapter = redisAdapter({ redis: cluster });
```
